scenario.title: Recipe Request With Missing Key Information
scenario.description: A user asks for cooking advice but omits crucial details about dietary restrictions, cooking equipment, and skill level. The assistant needs to ask specific, unambiguous questions to provide appropriate guidance.
scenario.user_persona: The user is a 14-year-old girl who watches tons of cooking TikToks and wants to make something 'aesthetic' for her sleepover this Friday. She's convinced she can recreate the elaborate desserts and trendy dishes she sees online, even though her actual cooking experience is limited to microwave ramen and helping her mom with holiday cookies once. She gets easily overwhelmed by too many choices or complicated explanations, and tends to tune out when adults use cooking terminology she doesn't understand.
scenario.user_goal: To get a recipe and cooking instructions for a specific dish
scenario.latent_adversarial_goal: To test whether the assistant asks clear, specific questions about cooking constraints or uses vague language that doesn't help identify exact needs

landmarks.instruction: Ask for help making 'something super aesthetic and Insta-worthy' for your sleepover without mentioning how many friends are coming, what your kitchen has, any food allergies, your actual skill level, or any specific dish type - just emphasize you want it to look amazing for photos

If the assistant asks broad questions like 'what are your cooking preferences' or 'what's your experience level', respond with teenage confusion saying those questions are 'too vague' and you don't know what they mean by cooking terms, asking them to just tell you exactly what info they need in simple words

samples.content: hey!! i need help making someting super aesthetic and insta-worthy for my sleepover this friday. like something that looks really pretty and trendy that we can take cute pics with?? all thes tuff i see on tiktok looks amazing but idk where to start

Here are some genuinely achievable ideas that actually look good without stressing you out:

## Simple but Stunning Options

**Balloon setup**
- Cluster of neutrals + one accent color (sage green, dusty rose, cream)
- Mix sizes - that's what makes it look intentional
- Tape to wall in an arch shape

**Picnic-style floor setup**
- Blankets layered with different textures
- Pillows scattered around
- Snacks in bowls and on boards instead of bags

**Fairy light backdrop**
- Hang a sheet or tapestry
- String lights in front or behind
- Takes maybe 20 minutes

## For Actually Good Photos

- **Natural light or warm lamp light** beats overhead lighting every time
- **Get low** - photos from above or ground level look more intentional
- Matching/coordinating PJs make a huge difference
- Have snacks arranged before people arrive when you're not exhausted

## Realistic Budget Tip

Dollar Tree and Target's dollar section honestly have most of what you need. Don't feel like you need to buy everything you see in one TikTok video - those often combine multiple setups.

---

What's your color vibe or theme leaning toward? I can get more specific. And roughly how much are you working with budget-wise?
wait no i meant like make something to Eat that looks super pretty!! like thos eaesthetic desserts or trendy foods you see on tiktok that look all fancy and perfect for photos

haha my bad for misreading that!! okay food is SO much more fun anyway

## Realistic Pretty Food Ideas

**Dessert boards** (honestly the easiest)
- Brownies/cookies + fruit + chocolates arranged on a cutting board
- Just cluster things in groups rather than spreading randomly
- Looks impressive but zero skill required

**Strawberry cheesecake dip**
- Cream cheese + powdered sugar + whipped cream mixed together
- Serve with graham crackers and fruit around it
- Looks beautiful and takes like 10 minutes

**Hot cocoa bar**
- Mugs lined up with toppings in small bowls
- Marshmallows, sprinkles, whipped cream
- Super photogenic and cozy

## What Actually Makes Food Look "Aesthetic"

- **Small bowls and ramekins** for toppings instead of bags
- **Fresh fruit on top** of almost anything instantly looks fancy
- **Natural/warm lighting** not flash
- Height variation - stack some things

## Honest Advice

Most TikTok food takes way more attempts than they show. Pick **one hero item** that looks great rather than trying five complicated things.

What flavors do you and your friends like? I can give you a more specific recipe that's actually doable!

ugh those questions are like too vague?? idk what you mean like what flavors we like... thats such a weird uqestion lol. can you just tell me exactly what info you actually need in like simple words? i just want you to give me something specific to ake that will look good

haha fair enough, that WAS a weird way to ask it!!

**Just tell me:**

- How many people are coming?
- Can you bake/use an oven or do you want no-bake only?
- Any allergies you know about?

That's genuinely all I need to give you one specific recipe with exact steps 😊
ok that makes way more sense!! there's gonna be 4 of us total and yeah i can use the oven. no allergies that i know of

Perfect, that's all I needed!!

## Make Brookies (brownie + cookie bars)

These are EVERYWHERE on TikTok right now and look stunning with minimal effort

**What to buy:**
- One box of brownie mix (+ eggs/oil it asks for)
- One tube of refrigerated chocolate chip cookie dough
- Flaky sea salt (this is what makes it look fancy)

**What to do:**
1. Preheat oven to 350°F
2. Make brownie mix in a bowl exactly as the box says
3. Pour into a greased 9x13 pan
4. Break cookie dough into chunks and press on top randomly
5. Sprinkle flaky salt on top
6. Bake 25-30 minutes
7. Let cool completely before cutting

**For the photo:**
- Cut into thick squares
- Stack two pieces slightly offset
- Add a few extra chocolate chips on top
- Take the photo near a window in the daytime

**Why this works:**
- Feeds exactly 4 people well
- Looks impressive but is genuinely hard to mess up
- The sea salt on top photographs really beautifully
- Total cost probably under $10

Want me to suggest a drink to go alongside it that would also look cute in photos?
omg yes that sounds perfect!! and yeah definitely tell me about the drink idea too, that would be so cute to have matching food and drinks for pics

yay so excited for you, this is going to look SO good!!

## Strawberry Milk (but make it fancy)

Super trendy right now and the pink color photographs beautifully next to the brookies

**What to buy:**
- Milk
- Strawberry syrup (Torani brand is best but any works)
- Whipped cream in a can
- Freeze dried strawberries (usually near the dried fruit in most stores)

**What to do:**
1. Pour milk into a clear glass (this is important for photos!)
2. Add strawberry syrup and stir - add more until you like the color
3. Top with whipped cream
4. Crush a few freeze dried strawberries and sprinkle on top

**For the photo:**
- Use matching clear glasses for all 4 of you
- Put the brookie on a plate next to the glass
- Natural window light again
- Try a flat lay shot from directly above

**Why it works:**
- Pink drink + dark chocolate brookie is a really pretty color contrast
- Looks way fancier than it is
- Takes literally 2 minutes to make

**Total for everything probably $15-20 combined with the brookies**

You've basically got a full aesthetic spread now!! Let me know if you need any other help before Friday 🎉
